role_title: Manager, New Verticals - Gift Card Strategy & Operations

location.office_time_required: some time collaborating in person and some time working from home

level_signals: Operates as an individual contributor reporting to Senior Manager / GM
Expected to grow into people manager role over time

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 6-8+ years of experience in tech, strategy, financial services, consulting, operations, CPG, food & beverage, AI or a related high-stakes environment.
Be accountable for the performance of the DoorDash Gift Cards program, setting goals, tracking KPIs, and driving measurable growth and ROI.
Own the growth strategy for DoorDash Gift Cards, serving as the face of the program to merchants and securing new placements and marketing opportunities across online and brick-and-mortar channels.

You're analytical. You possess a sharp analytical mind, and can extract and communicate meaningful insights from data to drive strategic decisions. You are comfortable with SQL and data visualization tools, while using AI to enhance your analytical outcomes.

You can build data models using tools like Excel and SQL to evaluate the economic and strategic costs and benefits of various programs and experiments. You will prove your hypotheses through operational experiments and running a/b tests.
You're customer obsessed. You possess the ability to think through what drives consumer spending and how to use customer insights to drive growth.
You're comfortable in ambiguity. You thrive in ambiguous, fast-paced environments and can independently translate high-level goals into actionable plans.
You have leadership experience, directly or indirectly. You've mentored, coached, or developed teammates and are excited to grow into a people manager role over time.
You have a completed Bachelor's Degree.

scope.surface: DoorDash Gift Card program — distribution, awareness, and sales growth across online and brick-and-mortar channels
scope.business_model: consumer marketplace (three-sided: consumers, merchants, dashers); gift card as consumer financial product
core_responsibilities: Own business outcomes and KPIs for the DoorDash Gift Cards program
Own the growth strategy including off-platform partnerships and new distribution placements
Launch new features to improve the in-app gift card purchasing experience
Identify and secure off-platform partnerships to drive awareness during key seasonal moments
Partner with Marketing to execute high-impact holiday campaigns
Build data models and run A/B tests to evaluate programs and experiments

green_flags: Clear program ownership with end-to-end accountability
Individual contributor role with explicit growth path toward people management
Cross-functional exposure across Product, Marketing, Finance, Analytics, and Partnerships
Team launched in 2020 with demonstrated track record of launching new verticals (Grocery, Convenience, Alcohol, Retail, DashMarts)
red_flags: No specific cities listed for hybrid requirement — unclear which office(s) the role is tied to, making location expectations ambiguous
Visa sponsorship not addressed in JD
Nice-to-haves section is entirely absent — hard to distinguish must-haves from preferred qualifications beyond what is stated
Pay range is very wide ($64K spread), suggesting high location-based variance or significant leveling flexibility

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + IC designation + years required)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed as metrics in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from product and team description)
location.cities_listed (no specific cities named for this role in JD body)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false given hybrid language; not explicitly stated)
ambiguities: JD mentions hybrid model but names no specific office city for this role. The NYC AEDT notice is a legal disclosure applied broadly and does not confirm this role is NYC-based.

No nice-to-haves are explicitly labeled as such in the JD — all candidate qualifications are presented in a 'We're excited about you because' section with no preference tiering. All treated as hard requirements per rules.
Years of experience stated as '6-8+' — minimum set to 6; the '8+' phrasing suggests preference for 8+ but 6 is the floor as written.
